draft.introduction: Ptolemy’s Almagest (c. 150 CE) is the comprehensive mathematical model of the geocentric universe that dominated astronomy for fourteen centuries. Its opening defends the value of mathematics as the truest form of knowledge.

translation.text: The true philosophers, Syrus, have rightly distinguished the theoretical part of philosophy from the practical. Of the theoretical, mathematics holds the first place, since it alone provides sure and incontrovertible knowledge. Theology deals with the invisible and unknowable; physics with the unstable and obscure; only mathematics can provide certain truth.

translator_notes.note: Ptolemy’s claim that mathematics provides the most certain knowledge, surpassing both theology and physics, is a bold philosophical position. The Almagest’s mathematical models predicted celestial positions with remarkable accuracy, which is precisely why the geocentric system proved so difficult to overthrow.
